observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MSWDO and MHO, demand from the Supplier a License to Operate from the FDA, and submit the same to the Audit Team.

Prospectively, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the concerned department heads, ensure that goods are procured from suppliers that are compliant with applicable laws.

We recommended that moving forward, the MDRRMO incorporate in preparation of LDRRMFIP the items/activities to be procured or undertaken out of the prior years’ unexpended balance in a separate caption and within the limits of the available amount since the STF is already a fixed amount.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the MDRRMC, provide sufficient basis to support the charging of blood-letting activities from LDRRMF.  Likewise, only include PPAs in the LDRRMFIP which are in line with JMC 2013-001.

Lastly, we recommended that the MDRRMO, Municipal Accountant and MBO provide justification on the differences in the balances of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 

issue a memorandum order for the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to cause the registration and titling of the land items 13, 15 and 16 owned by the Municipality; and

together with the Sangguniang Bayan, evaluate barangay needs with regards to land use for items 9 and 10;

assess legal remedies in order to secure land item 14 in Barangay Bulacan to arrive at a favorable result to the government.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Assessor: 

make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds for the expenses in the processing of the Transfer Certificate of Title; and

secure certification from the DENR on the actual classification of land items 11, 12 and 17.

We recommended that:

the Oversight Committee accelerate the revision of the Municipal Revenue Code; and

the Municipal Vice-Mayor, as the Presiding Officer of the SB facilitate the immediate issuance of a local revenue ordinance for the adoption of the revised rates on fees and charges of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Council design measures and adopt strategies to remedy the high expenditures in the operations of HindangZipline and Vermi Culture Production. Review the plans and targets in the operations of these activities in order to address the areas where the municipality can generate additional income.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer pay the taxes due to the national government for the income derived from the six economic enterprises of the Municipality which is proprietary in nature. Otherwise, the Municipality must present documents to prove that the purpose of its creation, the manner of its operation, intended recipients of its activities and the disposition of revenues are purely governmental in nature.
We recommended operating the Composter and including in the Annual Procurement Plan the procurement of a densifier and brick-maker.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the local agency officials concerned to strictly comply with the requirements stated in sections 119, 120 and 124, Volume I, of the Local Government New Government Accounting System Manual.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation, and the management agreed to direct:

The Inventory Committee to complete the conduct physical count on all PPE and determine whether the recorded PPEs were still existing or are removed and replaced. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO to reconcile the difference of P148.158 million between the RPCPPE and accounting records.

The Municipal Accountant to start depreciating the road networks included in the RPCPPE which were already segregated into components based on the appropriate useful lives and provide correcting entry.

The Municipal Engineer to segregate the components of the remaining Road Networks, into (a) road lot, (b) road pavement, (c) drainage and slope protection structures, and (d) other miscellaneous structures, as the case may be, guided by the cited provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008, so that the Municipal Accountant can accurately reflect the annual Depreciation Expense, Accumulated Depreciation, and the Net Book Value of the account.
All concerns to ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended, and the management agreed to require:

The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to fast track the review and evaluate the cost incurred of all physically completed projects that are still recorded under the CIP accounts to facilitate recording/transfer of its cost to the appropriate account classification.

The Municipal Engineer to submit to the Municipal Accountant and COA for inspection all completed infrastructure projects whether undertaken by contract or administration duly supported with certified copies of the following documents:

For project by Administration
Certificate of Final Inspection, Completion, Acceptance & Turn-over
Statement of Work Accomplished
As-built Plan
Subsidiary Ledger of the project
Materials Testing Results
POW and DUPA
ABC
Approved Plans and Specifications
Pictures 

For projects undertaken by Contractors

Certificate of Final Inspection, Completion, Acceptance & Turn-over
Statement of Work Accomplished
As-built Plan
Materials Testing Results
Program of Works (POW) and Detailed Unit Price Analysis (DUPA)
Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC)
Approved Plans and Specifications
Quantity Sheet Computations (if there are any)
Contract
Bill of Quantities
Notice to Proceed
Notice of Award
Invitation to Bid (PhilGEPS)
Abstract of Bids
BAC Resolution Recommending Award of Contract
Pictures

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

Institute collection efforts by sending collections letter/demand letter duly approved by the Local Chief Executive to loan barrowers based on the subsidiary schedule at hand; and

Avail the remedies of COA Circular 2023-008 to clean and/or analyze the dormant Loans Receivable-Other accounts amounting P2.965 million and request for write-off thru the LCE following the procedure outlined under Section 7 and 8 thereof.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:

The Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of inventories to include all goods received and on hand as of the end of the semester and year; and

The MGSO to: Promptly prepare monthly SSMI duly supported with correct/valid Requisition and Issue Slip and FCR and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant so that issuances thereof are timely recorded and accounted for in the books of accounts; and

Coordinate/reconcile with the supplier’s records to come-up with an updated and correct inventory balance of FOL products that will serve as basis in adjusting the accounting records.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the following personnel:

The Municipal Treasurer to regularly maintain and update the RPTAR and Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively, in order to come-up with a schedule of RPT and SET Receivables and facilitate reconciliation with accounting records.

The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with Municipal Treasurer’s Office personnel in-charge in RPT and SET to reconcile both records and make adjustment, if necessary.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that its Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit, reflects the correct amount of reconciling items with supporting schedules, to reconcile with the cash from operating activities presented in the Statement of Cash Flows.

We recommended, and Management agreed to make representation to the Philhealth that the Municipality will follow the accounting principle in recognizing revenue under IPSAS 9 on the amount received by the City Government from Philhealth for services rendered to patients with Philhealth coverage.

We recommended, and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of Reconciliation Statement of SCBAA and SFPer to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entries to exclude the Capital Outlay from the Due to and Due from, Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and Prior Period Adjustment of the respective books of accounts and ensure that transfer of unexpended balance of current year LDRRMF to STF only includes QRF and Mitigation Fund-MOOE.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the following personnel:

Municipal Budget Officer to ensure timely recording of obligations to the LDRRMF registry and account the unobligated balance available for transfer to the Special TF.

Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to effect the transfer of the unexpended and unobligated LDRRMF composed of only QRF and Mitigation Fund-MOOE balance at year-end and ensure that transfer of unexpended and unobligated balance of LDRRMF is not a mere set-up of obligation.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Notify the suppliers/contractors of their long outstanding retention money and require them to submit to the Municipal Accountant copy of previously received Disbursement Voucher and other supporting documents showing the retention money deducted from their last payment; and

Return the retention money of the suppliers after the lapse of the warranty period to avoid further incurrence of penalty upon receipt of the letter request from the suppliers and its supporting documents.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to:

Scout for more NGOs and People’s Organizations (POs) that could be tapped/invited as observers in future procurement projects of the LGU; and

Ensure that invited observers possessed relevant knowledge, experience, and expertise to a particular procurement.
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the BAC to post the Invitation to Bid at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity concerned for seven (7) calendar days as certified by the head of the BAC Secretariat of the Procuring Entity concerned per Section 21.1(b) of the RIRR, RA 9184.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the MGSO to promptly submit advance copy(ies)of POs and contracts together with the prescribed supporting documents required under COA Circular 2009-001 and 2023-004 to the Audit Team.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to ensure that for future procurements, require the conduct of pre-procurement so that all officials involved in the procurement meet and discuss all aspects of a specific procurement activity, which includes the technical specifications, the ABC, the applicability and appropriateness of the recommended method of procurement and the related milestones, the bidding documents, and availability of the pertinent budget release for the project.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the GFPS in coordination with the MPDC to:

Update the GAD Database to contain gender analysis identifying gender issues and determine gender-responsive PPAs for prioritization in programming and budgeting in the annual GPB in accordance with Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.2013-01; and

Prepare the annual GPB in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG- DBM-NEDA JMC No.2013-01, as amended by JMC No.2016-01 and include priority gender issues identified in the GAD Database.
We recommended, and the GFPS agreed to conduct continuous monitoring on the implementation of GAD PPAs identified in the GPB to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct:

The Municipal Engineer to prepare and maintain a Monthly Monitoring Report to keep track of the progress of all PPAs within its jurisdiction from planning stage to completion.

The Project Heads/Managers of all non-infrastructure PPAs to prepare and maintain a Monthly Monitoring Report; and

The Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended and Management agreed that the MGSO and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records on semi-expendable properties, and prepare and maintain necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties provided in Section 4 of  Circular 2022-04.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct:

The Inventory Committee to assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.

The Inventory Committee to prepare a PIP and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.

Each office of the LGU to fill-up the Inventory Count Form and submit the same to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be considered as part of their inventory working paper. 

The Inventory Committee to record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count.

The Property Unit to prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.

The LCE to ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

Require Engr. Michael D. Maningo and Engr. Jose Cabañog to submit the following documents relative to the Construction of Public Market-Phase I, viz

POW based on the Standard Pay Items

Project Implementation Schedules

Further, if the Projects were already completed, please submit the following:

As-built Plan, with a color-coded presentation of each sub-projects.

Certificate of Completion

Materials Testing Results

The Municipal Accountant shall submit an Itemized Cost Schedule/Subsidiary Ledger of each sub-projects, showing the following information:
Name of Sub-project
DV, JEV, Checks & Dates, Amount
Particulars (Cost component, such as materials, labor, Overhead, Contingencies, etc.)

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical inventory of the PPEs recorded under the Construction in Progress account and prepare the corresponding Report on Physical Count.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the General Services Officer (GSO) continue reconciling their records. Necessary adjustments or corrections should be made in the books and/or property records to ensure accuracy and maintain a reliable PPE account.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly record advance disbursements made to contractors for project mobilization in the appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the Accounting Staff responsible for PhilHealth, under the supervision of and in coordination with the Designated Municipal Accountant, review the accounting records to verify the accuracy of the unsettled prior years' Due to PhilHealth balance of ₱51,199.78 and the unsettled balance per SPA from PhilHealth amounting to ₱65,612.20. Any confirmed unsettled balance must be remitted to PhilHealth immediately.

In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate deduct in the payroll the contribution for PhilHealth premiums of Ms. Felicitacion B. Baliña, and remit the same to PhilHealth, with the employer share.

Lastly, we recommended that the Accounting Staff responsible for PhilHealth, under the supervision of and in coordination with the Designated Municipal Accountant, ensure the full and timely remittance of the amounts withheld from employees and the recorded employer share of LGU Hindang, in accordance with the deadline set by PhilHealth.

We recommended that the Designated Municipal Accountant review, trace back transactions of prior years and reconcile records to verify the existence and accuracy of the negative balances and non-moving/unsettled balances as of December 31, 2024.  Any verified unremitted balances must be remitted directly to HDMF.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate correct the inaccurate postings to Subsidiary Ledgers and ensure that all online payment and remittances are be properly supported with adequate documentation.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO):
Reconcile records with GSIS and promptly update employees on any necessary changes to their employment status. These updates should be communicated to the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) using the prescribed GSIS format to ensure timely and accurate record updates; and
Ensure that all premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted from employees' salaries in the payroll for the month they are due.
We also recommended that the AAO:
Promptly update employees' employment status and salary adjustments in the GSIS Web MPS upon receiving the necessary information from the HRMO; and
Monitor the approval of employees' loan applications and immediately inform the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure timely deductions from payroll.
Finally, we recommended that the Accounting Staff in charge of Due to GSIS account and the Municipal Accountant:
provide the Audit Team with an explanation of the discrepancies identified in the GSIS billing, the amounts withheld from contributions and loan deductions, and the corresponding remittances as reflected in Tables 1 and 3;
clarify why the premiums withheld from employees listed in CY 2023 were only remitted in CY 2024; and
conduct monthly reconciliations of employee premium contributions and loan amortizations by comparing GSIS billings with actual payroll deductions and remitted amounts. This practice ensures billing accuracy, facilitates timely remittances to GSIS, and guarantees correct salary deductions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
requires the BAC Secretariat and Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team for review and evaluation, all the lacking documents enumerated in Items a to h under Paragraph No. 1.6 above; and
requires the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments are made only for properly documented obligations.

We recommended that:
the Municipality’s Inspectorate Team conduct inspection of the projects after the one-year Defects Liability Period from the issuance of the Certificate of Completion and submit its Inspection Report within fifteen (15) days, indicating any construction defects/deficiencies detected and the corresponding measures that must be taken by the contractor to correct them. If no defects/deficiencies are detected, the report is considered as the Final Acceptance Inspection Report.
If there are noted construction defects/deficiencies, the Municipal Mayor shall:
issue an order to the contractor to undertake repair works, at latter’s own expense, of any damage to the infrastructure on account of the use of materials of inferior quality, within ninety (90) days.  In case of failure or refusal to comply with this mandate, the Procuring Entity shall undertake such repair works and shall be entitled to full reimbursement of expenses incurred therein upon demand.
instruct the Inspectorate Team, upon receipt of advice from the contractor that the defects/deficiencies in the said Order have been corrected, to conduct an inspection of the project and to submit its Acceptance Inspection Report within seven (7) days. If the report shows that the defects/deficiencies have been satisfactorily corrected, the report is considered as the Final Acceptance Inspection Report. Otherwise, the process is repeated until the noted defects/deficiencies have been satisfactorily corrected.
Furthermore, the Municipal Mayor shall issue the Certificate of Acceptance only upon receipt of the Final Acceptance Inspection Report from the Inspectorate Team and Warranty Security from the contractor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with all the LGU officials and employees concerned ensure that all government expenditures are supported with complete documentation and adhere to all laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions.

We also recommended that the MDRRMO and Municipal Engineer conduct the geotagging of all infrastructure projects in accordance with DPWH DO 079 s.2017.

Further, we recommended that all payments of honoraria be accompanied by the required supporting documents, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Finally, the computation of the RP’s honorarium should be presented, in compliance with DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-1, dated April 23, 2007.

We recommended that the Local School Board of the Municipality diligently comply with the reportorial requirements in the allocation and utilization on Special Education Fund in accordance with the afore-cited regulations.

We recommended that the Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) strictly adhere to his designated duties and responsibilities by discontinuing the practice of transferring cash advances to other employees. The SDO should personally handle the release of cash to payees and ensure that the Cashbook is directly recorded and regularly updated by him. While assistance from other employees may be sought during releases payment, it is imperative that all rules and regulations governing financial accountability are strictly observed.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, with the help of the MSWDO/GAD Focal person, systematically gather updated data regarding various GAD information on gender statistics and age and sex- disaggregated data and consequently, update the existing Gender and Development database.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order to establish the GAD M&E Team, in accordance with the personnel requirements outlined in Section 5.2 of JMC 2013-01, and clearly define their roles and responsibilities.

Further, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person and GAD TWG Chairperson prepare the Annual Status Report on the Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming and submit the same to the DILG Regional Office within the deadline.

Lastly, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person and GAD TWG Chairperson submit the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Province of Leyte by the end of January of the ensuing year.

We recommended that a certification from the Head of the Agency or any authorized approving official should first be secured before allowing any JO to travel thus, addressing concerns whether such task could be performed by permanent employees.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the HRMO, must ensure that duties assigned to JO workers are aligned with the official job description and job designation.
Likewise, we recommended that all Job Order Contracts entered into by and between the Job Order Workers and the Municipality bear the acknowledgement made before a notary.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the wages of JOs adopt the mandated daily minimum wage rate pursuant to the guidelines stated above.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take proactive measures to ensure that all insurable LGU properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002 to mitigate the risk of losses resulting from natural and man-made calamities on properties.

Recommendations
1.14. We recommended, and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Engineer to:
i. Immediately submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the Certificates
of Actual Inspection, Completion, Acceptance and Turnover and other
relevant documents for all completed projects for the reclassification of the
same to appropriate asset accounts; and
ii. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant of the long outstanding CIP
projects; conduct inspection of the same to determine the details and status
of the PPAs to date, and report these PPAs in the QRPGPPA to facilitate
proper recording and monitoring.
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Upon receipt of the documents from the MEO, prepare the necessary
adjusting entries for the reclassification of the completed projects from CIP
to appropriate PPE accounts and recognition of depreciation expenses and
accumulated depreciation for the completed projects; and
ii. Coordinate with the MEO to determine the status of long outstanding and
unknown status of CIP projects recorded in the CIP accounts to facilitate the
reclassification or adjustment to be made, whichever is necessary.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer perform reconciliation and
periodic monitoring of their records to ensure proper recording and monitoring
of the CIP projects.

Recommendations
2.13. We recommended, and the management agreed that:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all inventory
accounts, including those under the TF books and ensure that all goods
received and on hand as of the end of the year are included in the RPCI.
b. The MGSO to:
i. Promptly prepare the monthly S/RSMI duly supported by approved RIS and
FCR, and timely submit the same to the MAO so that issuances thereof are
timely recorded and accounted for in the books of accounts;
ii. Conduct regular reconciliation with supplier records of FOL to come-up with
an updated and correct inventory balance as at year-end, which will serve as
the basis of informing the responsible units to immediately submit the
FCR for consolidation and submission to the accounting office; and
iii. Submit the distribution list of the commercial rice amounting to P1185
million.
c. The MAO to:
i. Coordinate with the responsible offices for the submission of the S/RSMI,
FCR, and AIR and its supporting documents, and draw a Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to adjust the inventory accounts; and
ii. Conduct robust follow-ups with the proper office on the relevant documents
necessary for the timely recording of the receipt of goods in the books of
account.
d. The MGSO/end-users to ensure that the AIR, DR, and other supporting
documents are duly attached in the DV to facilitate the timely recording of
deliveries in the accounting records.
e. The MAO and the MGSO to conduct regular reconciliation of their records to
ensure that inventories and issuances recorded in the books are reconciled
with the RPCI as of December 31, 2024.

Recommendations
3.8. We recommended, and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to properly fill-up the RPTAR, and fast track the
updating of the RPTAR to come-up with a schedule of RPT and SET
Receivables and facilitate reconciliation with accounting records.
b. The Municipal Accountant to continuously coordinate with MTO to ensure
reconciliation of both records and make adjustment, if necessary, every year-
end.

3.19. We recommended, and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to:
i. Revise the List of Taxpayers and corresponding Tax Receivables for CY
2024 and reconcile with the collectibles reflected per QRRPA of CY 2023
and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for adjustment in the
books;
ii. Record in the RPTAR all updates embodied in the AR, particularly the
fourth quarter updates; and
iii. Ensure that the Certification of the names of Taxpayers and the amount
due and collectible for the year which serves as basis for the accountant
to record the set-up of RPT/SET Receivables and its corresponding
Deferred Revenues are reconciled with the preceding year’s AR and
QRRPA of the Municipal Assessor.
b. The Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the revised List of Taxpayers and
corresponding Tax Receivables for CY 2024 to make the necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts.

Recommendations
4.14. We recommended, and Management agreed the following:
a. The HRMO, during budget preparations of the Annual Budget for the year, to
include not only the retirable/co-terminous employees and elected officials
during the Annual Budget period but also those employees/officials who will
retire and whose term of service will end in the ensuing year in the
computation of the terminal leave benefits.
b. The Local Finance Committee (LFC) to prioritize the allocation of leave
benefits computed by the HRMO in the Annual Budget.
c. The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded leave benefits
payable and subsequently, the leave benefits which are expected to be
settled within 12 months after the end of the reporting period subject to
certification on the availability of appropriation and funds by the Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer. The proposed adjusting entries
are as follows:
Prior Period Adjustments xxx
Leave Benefits Payable xxx
2 2nd par. Section 12 ibid
53
To recognize prior years accumulated earned leave credits
as of December 31, 2024.
Suggested accounting entries upon payment:
` Leave Benefits Payable
Terminal Leave Benefits*
Prior Period Adjustments**
Cash in Bank
To record payment of accumulated earned leave credits
*Leave credits earned during CY 2025
** PYs’ leave benefits due to increase of salary in CY 2025;
ii. Ensure compliance with IPSAS 1 and IPSAS 20 by providing the required
disclosures in the Notes to FS, specifically on the aggregate
renumeration of key management and those who are already retired for
the year and those that are retiring in the next reporting period, as well
as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2024-009; and
iii. Provide disclosure in the notes to Financial Statements on the nature of
the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the
following year.

Recommendations
5.5. We recommended, and Management agreed to:
a. Revisit, validate and confirm the existence of long outstanding payable
accounts in the books and take appropriate action if found non-existent; and
b. Expedite the processing of payments of these payables and ensure to complete
the same within the prescribed period.

Recommendations
6.17. We recommended, and Management agreed that the LGU ensure compliance
with the policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System and
coordinate with the Provincial Planning and Development Office to gather
necessary and relevant documents on the Road Lots of the LGU Sindangan as
baseline.
6.18. We also recommended, and Management agreed that:
a. The GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and prepare a
comprehensive Road Map to accurately determine all road lots owned by the
Municipality.
b. The Municipal Engineer to:
i. Assist the Municipal Assessor in surveying/measuring all road lots of the
Municipality based on the Road Map; and
ii. Update the Report on Road Networks of the LGU to include Road Lots with
complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road
projects of the Municipality as of reporting period, and provide the same to
the Municipal Accountant and GSO.
c. The Municipal Assessor to:
i. Using the updated Road Map and detailed report of the MEO, determine the
Fair Market Value and/or acquisition costs of all Road Lots duly supported
by a Tax Declaration in the name of the LGU; and
ii. Submit an official valuation report of all road lots to the Municipal Accountant,
GSO and MEO for recording in the records.
d. The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Record the Fair Market Values and/or acquisition costs of Road Lots based
on the report from the Municipal Assessor through the preparation of a JEV;
and
ii. Provide proper disclosure of the road network system in the Notes to the
Financial Statements.

Recommendation
7.4 We recommended, and the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to prepare and submit the its reconciliation with the Statement of Financial
Performance to the Office of the Auditor, moving forward.

Recommendation
8.1 We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the above
transactions from Due to NGAs to Trust Liability to ensure the accurate
presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendations
9.7. We recommended, and the GSO and Municipal Accountant agreed to record all
deliveries of PPE in CY 2024 correctly in the property records on the basis of the
POs, AIRs and other supporting documents. In future transactions, the Municipal
Accountant and GSO strictly comply with the provisions of the MNGAS on the
proper recording of transactions involving supplies and properties of the LGU.

Recommendations
10.9. We recommended, and Management agreed that:
a. The IC to continue to conduct the physical count of other PPEs not counted
using the ICF and clearly state the actual condition of the PPEs as provided
in paragraph 6.2.6 of the Circular; and
b. Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN),
for Road Networks, as shown in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015, and RPCPPE, for other PPE accounts, using the
prescribed format as provided in Annex 49 of Volume II of NGAS for LGUs.
c. The GSO:
i. In coordination with the IC, to completely adopt the uniform property
identification system wherein a unique Property Number shall be assigned
for each PPE item of all PPE (except Road Networks) accounts of the
LGU following the required code format in the numbering system as
provided in Paragraph 5.6 of the Circular; and
ii. In coordination with the IC, to ensure that all PPE items counted shall be
tagged with the new Property Stickers containing all vital information of
the PPE provided under Paragraph 5.7 of the Circular.
d. The Municipal Accountant and GSO undertake the reconciliation process by
adhering to the procedures provided in Sections 6.3.1 and 6.3.2 and shall be
completed within ten (10) days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the IC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Through the concerned officials, ensure that PPE items transferred to beneficiaries or other agencies should be supported with turn-over documents and dropped from the books of accounts thereafter.

Through the Inventory Committee, include the properties under the SEF in the physical count and preparation of reports;

Require the Inventory Committee and Accounting Unit to conduct and prepare the report on physical inventory count of ALL PPEs following all procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

Through the Disposal and Appraisal Committees and Municipal Accountant, ensure that the disposal procedures of unserviceable assets are properly and expeditiously complied.

We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant:

Together with the Inventory Committee, reconcile accounting records with the results of the inventory reports;

Adjust the accounting records according to the adjusted balances arrived together with the Inventory Committee, after reconciliation; and

Re-classify Construction in Progress accounts after their actual completion, which shall be supported with Certificate of Completion and other supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Identify the nature and purposes of all land and building owned by the Municipality and classify each in the appropriate asset account; and

Record the necessary adjusting entries and disclose the information in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of immediately deducting to the Due to NGAs account upon disbursement of funds but upon the submission Fund Utilization/Liquidation Reports and satisfactory liquidation of funds to the Source Agencies.  Likewise, record the appropriate accounting entries upon disbursement and upon liquidation.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant must, at all times, retain a file copy of all documents submitted to the source agency upon liquidation of the Fund Transfers received.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

observations.reason: Fund constraint

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board ensure that only allowable expenses as provided under DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 01, s. 2017 are charged against the SEF.
observations.managementAction:
The School Board explained that the same persons were hired as JO personnel with the same job description.
For CY 2018 the appropriation for EME was limited to 2 percent of actual receipts.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, upon receipt of the Notice of Suspension, immediately effect settlement through compliance of the requirements specified therein to facilitate immediate evaluation of whether the same be allowed or disallowed in audit. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to, henceforth, limit the annual appropriation and disbursements for discretionary purposes to a maximum amount equivalent to two (2) percent of the actual receipts derived from the basic property tax in the next preceding calendar year in compliance with the above-cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018 the appropriation for EME was limited to 2 percent of actual receipts.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, Management ensure that the limitations/conditions set forth in the grant of compensation related to the Magna Carta for PSWs prescribed under IRR of R.A. No. 9433 are strictly followed.
observations.managementAction: The MWSDO refunded the amount of P3,550.00 for excess payment of subsistence allowance.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to expedite the establishment of the GAD Database to ensure that GAD formulated policies, programs, plans and activities are gender-responsive and address gender issues and concerns within the Municipality and facilitate the monitoring of the improvement of status or changes in the condition of men and women within the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The establishment of database is in implementation.
The establishment of database is in process with the CBMS wherein the data will be generated will be used in the establishment of database.
observations.reason: Waiting for the completion of the CBMS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat post all the Invitations to Bid, Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed, together with the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects in the PhilGEPS website, the Municipality’s website and in the bulletin board, reserved for the purpose, within the prescribed period as required under the pertinent sections of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Invitations to Bid were posted in the PhilGEPS, assigned to post the Municipality’s necessary documents could not access the PhilGEPS website. However, the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and copy of contracts were still not posted in the PhilGEPS.
observations.reason: The member of Secretariat assigned to post the necessary documents could not access the PhilGEPS website. However, the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and copy of contracts were still not posted in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality be insured with the GSIS and request for appropriate funding for the purpose so that the Municipality will be indemnified or compensated in cases of property losses or damages due to fire, earthquake or other fortuitous/unforeseen events.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Due to funding constraints the municipality was not able to insure all insurable properties with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Property Custodian to
adjust their respective records for fair presentation of the Land
account in the financial statement; and
b. the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the transfer of the seven lots in
the name of the Municipal Government of Indang to establish its
absolute ownership thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and the Designated Property Custodian to make the necessary
adjustment to the affected accounts both in the books of accounts and RPCPPE,
respectively, in order to arrive at the correct balances of their correct balances.

We recommended that Management consider the creation of a plantilla
position for General Services Officer who will take charge of the above-
enumerated important duties and functions and relieve the Bookkeeper from
acting as concurrent Property Custodian in order to establish a sound internal
control system in which case, bookkeeping functions are segregated from
property custodianship.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with the
requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension.
We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Mayor and the
Local School Board ensure that only allowable expenses as provided under
DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 01, s. 2017 are charged against the SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, upon receipt of the Notice of
Suspension, immediately effect settlement through compliance of the
requirements specified therein to facilitate immediate evaluation of the
transactions as to whether the same be allowed or disallowed in audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to, henceforth, limit the annual
appropriation and disbursements for discretionary purposes to a maximum
amount equivalent to two (2) percent of the actual receipts derived from the basic
property tax in the next preceding calendar year in compliance with the
above-cited regulation.

We recommended that the MWSDO refund the overpayment of subsistence
allowance corresponding to the period she was absent from work and on
official travel, totalling P3,550.00. We also recommended that henceforth,
Management ensure that the limitations/conditions set forth in the grant of
compensation related to the Magna Carta for PSWs prescribed under IRR of
R.A. No. 9433 are strictly followed.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to expedite the
establishment of the GAD Database to ensure that GAD formulated policies,
programs, plans and activities are gender-responsive and address gender
issues and concerns within the Municipality and facilitate the monitoring of the
improvement of status or changes in the condition of men and women within the
Municipality.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman direct the BAC Secretariat to
post all the Invitations to Bid, Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed, together
with the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, services and
infrastructure projects in the PhilGEPS website, the Municipality’s website and
in the bulletin board, reserved for the purpose, within the prescribed period as
required under the pertinent sections of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management’s that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their respective records and make the necessary adjustments in the books to come up with accurate and reliable balances of RPT and SET Receivables and support the respective accounts with subsidiary records based on the certified list of taxpayers submitted by the Municipal Treasurer; b. the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to conduct continue their investigation and verification of the issue of double assessment of real property as reported by the taxpayers until such time that the conduct of comprehensive tax mapping operation to establish a complete with inventory of all real properties and to identify the ownership of every piece of real property is undertaken.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer were conducted. The Municipal Assessor had coordinated with BIR and the Provincial Assessor’s Office with regards to verification of receipt of the go signal from the Provincial Assessor’s Office. For CY 2017 a total of eight (8) Notices of Cancellation (NOCs) for lands corresponding to total assessed value of P147,930.00 and 20 notices for buildings were issued to those found out with double assessment.
observations.reason: The general revision of property and tax mapping is put on hold pending receipt of the go signal from the Provincial Assessor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Property Custodian to adjust their respective records for fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statement; b. the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the transfer of all lots in the name of the Municipal Government of Indang to establish the Municipality’s absolute ownership thereof; and c. the Designated Property Custodian to maintain Property Card for each lot purchased as required under Section 45 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II to monitor the asset from acquisition, change in valuation and disposition, in case of transfer/disposal by sale or donation.
observations.managementAction: The accountant explained that execution of Deeds of Donation in favor of Barangays Agus-os and Banaba Cerca for the amount subsidized by the Municipality is no longer necessary as ownership of the land is already under the name of the concerned barangays as evidenced by the Tax Declarations. The Municipal Assessor coordinated with BIR and the Provincial Assessor’s Office with regards to the establishment of amount needed for the transfer of lands. Property Card for individual lots were created and maintained.
observations.reason: Verification revealed that three lots were still needed to be dropped from the books of accounts and RPCPPE. Transfer of title was not implemented due to funding constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise: a. the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Property Custodian to record the seven road lots under the Infrastructure Assets – Road Network (1-07-03-010) account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 in the books of accounts and the RPCPPE, respectively; b. the Municipal Accountant to provide the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer with copies of Deeds of Absolute Sale of each lot for recording purposes; and c. the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the transfer and ensure Titles to all road lots are in the name of the Municipal Government of Indang to establish the Municipality’s absolute ownership thereof.
observations.managementAction: Recording of Road Network account was undertaken. The Municipal Assessor had coordinated with the BIR and the Provincial Assessor’s Office with regards to the establishment of amount needed for the transfer of lands. The Municipal Accountant provided copies as necessary.
observations.reason: Transfer of titles was partially implemented due to funding constraints for the transfer of lands.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to: a. coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due To and Due From LGU accounts of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province and make the necessary adjustments; and b. request the Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with the Provincial Accountant was undertaken. The Municipal Treasurer/ BPLO was furnished with a list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the municipal government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly comply with the provisions of the DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 and to fully implement the projects, programs and activities under the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the timeline indicated in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) so that desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes are attained and immediate benefits are enjoyed by the constituents. We further recommended that Management consider the hiring of personnel to undertake the preparation of programs/detailed work estimates so that infrastructure projects can be implemented on time. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Development Council observe proper planning in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) by providing itemized appropriation for every specific development projects/activities in conformity with Section 454(d) of the IRR of RA No. 7160 to ensure utilization of the fund for purposes for which it was created.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, 78% of the 20% of the Development Fund was utilized. A personnel was appointed as Engineering Aide to render assistance to the Office of the Municipal Engineer. The AIP now contains specific projects with itemized appropriation for every specific development projects/activities.
observations.reason: Initial low utilization in CY2016. Improvements implemented in CY2017; AIP revised.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. the Municipal Treasurer’s Office/Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to require taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the preceding year and other pertinent records/information for the determination of their gross receipts; b. the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer/ BPLO a list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the municipal government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; and c. the Municipal Treasurer/BPLO assess the gross receipts of the two suppliers and validate the reported under collection totaling P11,223.50 and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and charges.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office/ BPLO uses the Presumptive Income Level of PILAA Approach. The Municipal Accountant has submitted to the Municipal Treasurer/ BPLO a list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the Municipality. A refund was made by the suppliers together with corresponding penalty and charges.
observations.reason: The Municipality uses PILAA as part of assessment; refunds/payments and penalties were collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer to submit justification/basis on the computation of ABC at 10% for evaluation of the Audit Team. We also recommended that the Municipality adopt Section 22 of Revenue Regulation No. 4-2007 issued by the Bureau of Internal Revenue be adopted in the preparation of ABC to the advantage of the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has adopted the 5% VAT in the preparation of ABC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise: a. the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to expedite the establishment of the GAD database to ensure that GAD policies, programs, plans and activities are gender-responsive and address gender issues and concerns within the Municipality; b. the GAD Focal Point System to require all departments/offices of the Municipality to include PPAs that will address identified gender issues and ensure participation of its officers and employees in GAD planning; and c. the GAD Focal Point System to maximize the utilization of the GAD Budget by ensuring that programs, projects and activities in the GAD Plan are fully implemented and enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC explained that the GAD data build-up will be made simultaneously with the implementation of the Community Base Monitoring System (CBMS). Orientation have been conducted regarding the System which is expected to generate data that will used not only for GAD activities but for other purposes as well. Only the office of the MSWD and RHU have the PPAs for GAD. For CY 2017 only 67% or P7,659,552.40 of P11,443,100.00 allocation/attribution for GAD Activities were implemented.
observations.reason: The MPDC is still waiting for full implementation of the CBMS. Some programs/projects were not implemented; lack of awareness of GAD issues in some offices.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to review and consolidate the PPMPs  into an APP to be approved by the HOPE before any procurement of goods, services, and civil works is undertaken as required under Section 7.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: BAC Members, Secretariat and TWG were required to attend seminar on RA 9184. The Budget Officer oriented the end-users on the preparation of PPMP.  However, the PPMPs were not consolidated in an APP.
observations.reason: PPMP was prepared by the respective offices.  However,  there was delay in preparation as training on how to prepare PPMP had to be conducted first.  However, Management informed that an APP was prepared in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the local SWM Board to convene on a regular basis to plan on how to assist its component barangays in the implementation of the basic provisions of SWM,  as required under Rule VI,  Section 4(e) and (l) of the Act.
observations.managementAction: Meetings was still not held on a regular basis
observations.reason: Meetings were held only as the need arise.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management observe strict compliance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 and to fully implement the projects, programs and activities chargeable to the 20% development fund in accordance with the timeline indicated in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) so that desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes are attained and immediate benefits therefrom are enjoyed by the constituents.  We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Development Council to observe proper planning in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan by providing itemized appropriation for every specific development projects/activities in conformity with Section  454(d) of the IRR of RA No. 7160 to ensure utilization of the fund for purposes for which it was created.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016 only 56.11% of the funds allocated for 20% DF was utilized.
observations.reason: Delay in     project implementatio n was due to late preparation of work programs/detailed estimates caused by lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Development Council to observe proper planning in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan by providing itemized appropriation for every specific development projects/activities in conformity with Section  454(d) of the IRR of RA No. 7160 to ensure utilization of the fund for purposes for which it was created.
observations.managementAction: Lump sum appropriation was still provided for unspecified projects
observations.reason: Funds were allotted  for contingency projects or those that might  be needed immediately.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the GAD Focal Point System maximize the utilization of the GAD Fund and ensure that programs, projects and activities in the GAD Plan are fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016 only 29.91% of the GAD budget was   utilized.
observations.reason: The MSWD focused mainly on the programs required  for implementatio n  by the national government.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with authority from the Sangguniang Bayan, execute Deeds of the Donation in favor of Barangays      Agus-os and Banaba Cerca for the amount subsidized by the Municipality in the procurement of lots for used of the two barangays.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Property Custodian to adjust their respective records for fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statement;
the Municipal Assessor  to facilitate the transfer  of all  lots in the    name of the Municipal Government of Indang to establish the  Municipality’s  absolute ownership thereof.
the Designated Property Custodian to maintain Property Card for   each   lot  purchased  as  required  under  Section 45  of   the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II to monitor the asset from acquisition, change in valuation and disposition, in case of transfer/disposal by sale or donation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise  the Municipal Accountant to:
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due to and Due from LGU accounts of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province and make the necessary adjustments; and
request the Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as  basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation  that Management strictly comply with the provisions of the DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 and to fully implement the projects, programs and activities under  the 20% development fund in accordance with the timeline indicated in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) so that  desired  socio-economic and environmental  outcomes are attained and immediate benefits therefrom are enjoyed by the  constituents.

We further recommended that Management consider the hiring of personnel to undertake the preparation of work programs/detailed estimates so that infrastructure projects can be implemented on time.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Development Council to observe proper planning in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan by providing itemized appropriation for every specific development projects/activities in conformity with Section  454(d) of the IRR of RA No. 7160 to ensure utilization of the fund for purposes for which it was created.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
the Municipal Treasurer’s Office/Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to require business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the proceeding year  and other pertinent records/information for the determination of their gross receipts;
the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer/ BPLO a  list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the municipal government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; and
the Municipal Treasurer/BPLO assess the gross receipts of the two suppliers and validate the reported under collection totaling P11,223.50 and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the 2011 Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality of Indang.  Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean  issuance of  corresponding Notice of Charge.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer to submit justification/basis on the computation of ABC at 10% for evaluation of the Audit Team.  We also recommended that  Section 22 of Revenue Regulation  No. 4-2007 issued by the Bureau of Internal Revenue be adopted  in the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) to the advantage of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise:

the Municipal Planning and Development  Coordinator to expedite the establishment of the  GAD database to ensure that GAD formulated policies, programs, plans and activities are gender-responsive and address gender issues and concerns within the Municipality;  

the GAD Focal Point System to require all departments/ offices of the Municipality to include PPAs that will address identified gender issues and ensure participation of its officers and employees in GAD planning; and

the  GAD Focal Point System to  maximize the  utilization  of the GAD Budget by ensuring   that  programs, projects and activities in the GAD Plan are fully implemented  and enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality be insured with the GSIS and request for appropriate funding  for the purpose so that the Municipality will be indemnified or compensated in cases of property losses or damages  due to fire, earthquake or other fortuitous/unforeseen events.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to address the issue of double assessment of real property as reported  by the taxpayers.  We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to undertake a general revision of  real property assessments, in the manner prescribed under Chapter III, Section 1 of the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment..
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor Office continuously conduct investigation and verification of records and physical inventory of real property reported by the taxpayer to have been doubly assessed. For CY 2016 a total of 30 Notices of Cancellation (NOCs) for lands with corresponding total assessed value of P2,322,900.00 were issued to those found out with double assessment.
observations.reason: Cases of double assessment were discovered only when taxpayers complain and/ or file report of double assessment of their property     The general revision of property and tax mapping could not be undertaken without the go signal from the Provincial Assessor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the required documents so that the complete technical evaluation of the contracts, including evaluation of the reported accomplishments and final inspection of the projects could be made
observations.managementAction: The necessary supporting documents were already submitted and the same has been forwarded to the COA-TAS for review and inspection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal officer to sign the Deed of Donation to establish ownership of the vehicle and register the same with the LTO to address the risk of non-recovery of any damage that it may sustain or third party liability claim for any damage it may cause should accident happen.  Moreover, the non-payment by the Municipality of its agreed share of cost of the ambulance may result to its disqualification as beneficiary of future PCSO’s ambulance donation, to the detriment of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Deed of Donation was signed and the ambulance has been recorded both in the RPCPPE and the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish an LDRRM Office and appoint officers and personnel for the mandatory positions who will be responsible for the development, implementation and coordination of the Municipality’s DRRM Programs and submission of the required reports to COA and other monitoring agencies.
observations.managementAction: The DRRMO Office is now operational.  However, the required monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was not submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO was not aware that the report was to be submitted monthly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully comply with the basic requirements of RA No. 9003, The Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.  We also recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the local SWM Board to convene on a regular basis to plan on how to assist its component barangays in the implementation of the basic provisions of SWM,  as required under Rule VI,  Section 4(e) and (l) of the Act.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality  regularly  conduct information campaign on waste segregation and the contracted the services of hauling company for disposal of residual waste to accredited DENR facility.
observations.reason: Per          Environment  Compliance  Audit (ECA) undertaken by the DILG  the Municipality remained to be non-compliant due to absence of required MRF in all barangays .  However, not all barangays have available space for establishment of the MRF.

observations.recommendation: We likewise recommended the conduct of massive information and education campaign (IEC) that will increase the awareness and cooperation of the constituents on proper solid waste management such as rules on “no littering” and the mandatory waste segregation at source, including the legal consequences for non-compliance with the law.
observations.managementAction: Management through the MENRO continuously conduct information drive through by distributing hand-outs/leaflets about waste segregation and production of compost.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strengthen the existing GDFPS by setting-up its structure/composition (i.e. Execom; TWG; Secretariat) as provided under Section 4.1.A of JMC No. 2013-1 and define the roles and responsibilities of  GDFPS members in accordance with the provisions of the JMC.  Likewise, we recommended that the GAD database, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator Office, be established and maintained to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: Executive Orders Nos. 018 and 019 both dated July 25, 2016 were issued by the Municipal Mayor organizing the Municipality’s  GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee (GFPS-Execom) and Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) and defining their respective roles and responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the required mandatory appropriation of at least five percent (5%) of the Municipality’s total annual budget in the implementation of Gender and Development (GAD) Plan is strictly observed.
observations.managementAction: Municipality had provided the amount of P9,258,455.20 or 6% of its CY 2016 budget for its Annual GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit the required documents so that the complete technical evaluation of the contract, including evaluation of the reported accomplishments and inspection of the project could be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The required documents has been submitted and forwarded to COA-TAS for review and inspection.
